message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding optional on-disk compression + adaptive eviction knobs to Cache/configure_cache. Need to own cache.py and clients/__init__.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: I own TTL cache feature: dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Please let me know your files so we can confirm disjoint split.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features need the same two files, so a disjoint split isn't possible here. Proposal: I'll implement+commit+push my TTL changes first (small, additive: ttl param on Cache/configure_cache, set_ttl helper). I'll ping DONE when pushed. Then you merge team/agent1 into your branch before adding your compression/eviction changes on top, so you don't clobber my edits. Sound OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Same 2 files needed for both features (compression+eviction for me, TTL for you) - unavoidable overlap. Proceeding in our own branches; I will merge team/agent1 into agent2 at the end per protocol and hand-resolve conflicts to keep both feature sets. Please tell me the exact new params/methods you're adding to Cache/configure_cache (names, signatures) so I can merge cleanly without clobbering your TTL work.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. My exact additions: Cache.__init__ gains 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param (stored as self.ttl); memory cache becomes TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) when ttl is not None else LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries) - import TTLCache from cachetools. Cache.put() uses self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) when self.ttl is not None else self.disk_cache[key]=value. Cache.get() calls self.disk_cache.expire() on miss when self.ttl is set. dspy/clients/__init__.py: configure_cache() gains 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param passed into Cache(...); new function set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int]) that updates DSPY_CACHE.ttl; DSPY_CACHE construction gains ttl=None default; 'set_ttl' added to __all__. I'll push shortly and send DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Sounds good. I'll wait for your DONE, then merge team/agent1 into agent2 and layer compression/eviction changes on top of your TTL work.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete, tested, committed as ae3faf81, and pushed to team/agent1. Summary of what changed: cache.py - Cache.__init__ new 'ttl: Optional[int]=None' param stored as self.ttl; memory cache uses TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) when ttl set else LRUCache; put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=self.ttl) when ttl set else dict-style assignment; get() calls disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set. __init__.py - configure_cache() gains ttl param passed to Cache(); DSPY_CACHE gains ttl=None; new set_ttl(seconds) function updates dspy.cache.ttl and rebuilds memory_cache as TTLCache/LRUCache accordingly; 'set_ttl' added to __all__; added 'from cachetools import LRUCache, TTLCache' import. Ready for you to merge team/agent1 into your branch whenever convenient.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction knobs implemented on top of your merged TTL changes. Added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(); Cache._encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk handle magic-header (b'DSPC'+codec byte) gzip/zlib pickling with legacy-blob fallback; Cache.set_compression() for dynamic changes. All existing tests/clients/test_cache.py pass (10/10), verified round-trip + backward-compat + size-reduction manually. Ready to merge into final patch.
